input.background: A study of observer variation was performed using radiographic diagnosis of caries on 3869 molars and premolars (Espeland and Handelman, 1989). The results for three dentists are shown in a table. Teeth were diagnosed as sound (S) or carious (C).
input.question: Which pair of dentists agreed best?

output.answer:
For each pair of dentists, each tooth can be categorized as SS, SC, CS, or CC by aggregating the relevant rows of the table. The resulting $2 \times 2$ tables can be assessed by calculating kappa, as follows:

\begin{tabular}{llrrrll}
\hline & SS & \multicolumn{1}{c}{ SC } & \multicolumn{1}{c}{ CS } & CC & kappa & \%agree \\
\hline $1 \vee 2$ & 3520 & 280 & 123 & 216 & 0.46 & $97 \%$ \\
$1 \vee 3$ & 2182 & 1348 & 43 & 296 & 0.18 & $64 \%$ \\
$2 \vee 3$ & 2164 & 1209 & 61 & 435 & 0.26 & $67 \%$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Also shown is the percentage agreement. Thus, the best agreement is between dentists 1 and 2. Dentist 3 considered that there were many more carious teeth (1644) than the other two observers (339 and 496).
